description: Tracks all file changes between Cowork sessions and GitHub pushes. Each session entry records what was added, modified, or deleted — and whether it has been pushed to the live repo.

workflow.step_1: Claude makes changes in HUF-repo/ during a Cowork session
workflow.step_2: Changes are logged here with status 'staged'
workflow.step_3: Peter copies changed files to D:\GitHub\HUF and pushes via GitHub Desktop
workflow.step_4: Peter (or Claude) updates status to 'pushed' with commit hash
workflow.note: If a session ends before push, this manifest tells you exactly what's pending

sessions.label: Q-Inquisitor concept + 3^n Confidence Index + Ramsar Complexity Gap + Governance Declaration (dirty laundry)
sessions.suggested_commit_message: New concepts: Q-Inquisitor (Small's Q for compositional coupling), 3^n Confidence Index (opinion→agreement→validation), Ramsar Complexity Gap (10 deployment gaps), Governance Declaration (8 known unknowns, 5 doubts, full transparency)
sessions.notes: Peter's review-day rant produced four interlocking documents. Q_INQUISITOR.md: HUF as intersystem quality diagnostic rooted in Richard H. Small's Q parameters (1972) — measures coupling quality at boundaries, not performance within subsystems. CONFIDENCE_INDEX.md: 3^n framework where n=1 is opinion (3 checks), n=2 is agreement (9 checks), n=3 is validation (27 checks) — HUF's three diagnostics provide exactly n=3 minimum for Ramsar deployment. RAMSAR_COMPLEXITY_GAP.md: 10 specific gaps between HUF capability and Ramsar deployment (3 CRITICAL, 3 SERIOUS, 4 MODERATE) — every critical gap requires CoDa or Ramsar collaboration. GOVERNANCE_DECLARATION.md: the dirty laundry — 8 known unknowns (KU-01 through KU-08), 5 doubts (D-01 through D-05), declaration that transparency is non-negotiable. Together these form the self-assessment layer: HUF measuring HUF.

files.summary: HUF as Q-inquisitor: intersystem quality diagnostic rooted in Small's Q parameters. Measures coupling at boundaries between compositional subsystems. CR/Aitchison as compositional analogue of 1/Q. Three inquisitor questions: is coupling clean, where is the leak, is it growing? Proposes 'Aitchison-Small Diagnostic' naming.

3^n Systems Confidence Index. n=0 assertion, n=1 opinion (3 checks), n=2 agreement (9 checks), n=3 validation (27 checks), n=4 certification (81 checks), n=5 standard (243 checks). HUF at n=2 with dual metric, n=3 with coherence residual. Ramsar deployment path to n=4 and n=5. Honest caveat: framework itself is at n=1.

10-gap register: GAP-01 data heterogeneity (CRITICAL), GAP-02 zero prevalence at scale (CRITICAL), GAP-03 temporal resolution (SERIOUS), GAP-04 SBP design for ecology (SERIOUS), GAP-05 cross-site comparability (SERIOUS), GAP-06 reporting bias (MODERATE), GAP-07 computational scale (MODERATE), GAP-08 institutional adoption (MODERATE), GAP-09 CoDa acceptance (MODERATE), GAP-10 unknown unknowns (CRITICAL). No gap is HUF-internal only.

The dirty laundry. What HUF is and is not (5 'is not' declarations). 8 known unknowns: KU-01 compositional validity of ecological data, KU-02 diagnostic independence, KU-03 coherence residual interpretation, KU-04 Greenacre bridge honesty, KU-05 loudspeaker-to-ecology transfer, KU-06 AI collective strength/liability, KU-07 catalogue completeness, KU-08 confidence framework validity. 5 doubts: white flag posture, integrate vs branch, Ramsar prematurity, screwdriver framing, over-documentation. 5 beliefs despite doubts. Open invitation.

Added the_third_diagnostic conceptual development to key_conceptual_developments. Updated corpus_state with S012 counts. Conference readiness updated with scaling_solution and third_diagnostic entries. Corpus count updated to 295. One-sentence summary expanded.
S012 updated with SCALING_COHERENCE.md, THE_THIRD_DIAGNOSTIC.md, WHAT_HUF_IS.md origin rewrite. S013 entry added. File count tracking updated.

sessions.label: CGS + Coherence Residual + Backblaze cross-domain + Peterson v7 + CRPT-008
sessions.suggested_commit_message: Add SYSTEM_MAP.md (cognitive compression), S013_DOCUMENT_COMPARISON.xlsx (1/Q insight), CMSI.md (Kardashev-scale target for compositional governance)
sessions.notes: Three outputs from April 5 review day. (1) Peter hit cognitive Nyquist (E-15 on himself) — SYSTEM_MAP.md created as 5-pillar lossless compression with zoom protocol. (2) S013_DOCUMENT_COMPARISON.xlsx: 3-sheet comparison with the 1/Q insight. (3) CMSI.md: Complex Mixed Systems Index — a Kardashev-scale logarithmic target for compositional governance. Base quantity: Governed Degrees of Freedom (GDoF = carriers × sites × diagnostics × temporal_scales, summed across domains). CMSI = log₁₀(GDoF). Five levels defined target-first: Level 0 (proof of concept, <100 GDoF) through Level 4 (universal standard, >100M GDoF). HUF today: CMSI 1.68 (Level 0). Full Ramsar: CMSI 6.78 (Level 3). Gap: 5.1 orders of magnitude. Two orthogonal axes: CMSI (how much governed) × n (how well validated). Backblaze becomes non-optional — cross-domain is the only path to Level 1. Ashby's law exactly satisfied at full Ramsar scale.

files.summary: Lossless compression instrument for the human operator. 5 pillars (Origin, Instrument, Math, Deploy, Honesty), Level 0-3 zoom protocol, process layer, key metrics table. 'The system that monitors compositions should be able to monitor itself.'

3-sheet Excel: (1) Document Comparison — 4 S013 docs across 28 dimensions in 7 categories, (2) The 1/Q Insight — Small's Q → compositional analogue CR/Aitchison, domain-independent coupling quality, (3) Self-Assessment Summary — all dirty laundry colour-coded by severity. Confidence Level: n=1 (Opinion).

Complex Mixed Systems Index — Kardashev-scale logarithmic target for compositional governance. CMSI = log₁₀(GDoF). Five levels. HUF today: CMSI 1.68 / Level 0. Full Ramsar: CMSI 6.78 / Level 3. Kardashev V target: CMSI 8.0. Includes NASA SCM interface-counting precedent, 7-stage geometric expansion (point → line → triangle → tetrahedron → polytope → polytope network → universal), full system assessment (48+ documented misses), Ashby's law verification, Earth-scale GDoF calculation (~10.8M across 7 domains).

3-sheet Excel workbook. Sheet 1 'Full System Assessment': W-01–W-05 (what works), M-01–M-05 (critically missing), X-01–X-05 (might be wrong), Z-01–Z-07 (zeros that matter), summary counts with formulas. Sheet 2 'Geometric Expansion': 7-stage table (point → universal polytope network) with geometry, HUF examples, CMSI values, NASA analogues. Sheet 3 'Action Priority': A-01–A-08 matrix with timelines (TODAY/THIS WEEK/BEFORE COIMBRA), what each resolves, success criteria. Recalculated with 0 formula errors.

The Compositional Governance Scale (CGS) — supersedes CMSI levels. Renames CMSI to CGS for scientific clarity. Same base quantity (GDoF) and formula (CGS = log₁₀(GDoF)). Five levels (CGS-1 through CGS-5), each gated by four requirements: threshold, structure, evidence, institution. CGS-1 (Instrument Proof) satisfied. CGS-2 (Cross-Domain Validation) is Coimbra target — requires Backblaze PoC + CR computation + peer presentation. CGS-3 (Operational Deployment) requires Ramsar pilot, ~2028-2030. CGS-4 (Network Governance) requires full Ramsar + ISO standard, ~2032-2038. CGS-5 (Universal Standard) requires all compositional domains, treaty-level, 2040+. Includes Ashby verification at each level, realistic timeline, governance state notation (CGS-k, n), naming convention for publication. Confidence: n=1.

First empirical computation of the coherence residual on EMBER. 74 transitions across 3 countries, 8 SBP nodes each. Full results: balance deltas, CR per node, pattern classification, key event analysis. Method: ILR balance decomposition, cross-branch normalised coupling indicator, multiplicative zero replacement.

First empirical results of the third diagnostic on EMBER. Key findings: strict coherence does not hold (mean CR ~0.58 across all sites), all four agreement patterns appear, STRUCTURAL pattern is 31% of transitions (invisible structural change), LOCAL_EVENT is rarest (1.4% — almost nothing happens in isolation in energy systems). Key events confirmed with new coupling information: Fukushima (CR=0.742, gas/fossil coupling), German nuclear exit (CR=0.646, renewable mix restructuring), UK coal collapse (CR=0.528, root balance shift). HUF on EMBER moves to (CGS-1, n=3). First quantitative evidence on Egozcue-Greenacre question.

Backblaze cross-domain proof-of-concept. 900,000+ drives across 4 manufacturer families (Seagate, WD, Toshiba, HGST), 8 quarters (Q1 2024–Q4 2025), 5-carrier SMART health composition (Healthy, Degraded_Sectors, Degraded_Errors, Degraded_Both, Failed). Three diagnostics applied. 35 transitions. HGST shows 100% coupled events. Cross-domain CR mean ~0.59–0.69. Combined GDoF = 264, CGS = 2.42. CGS-2 threshold reached.

Peterson letter v7.0. Updated with: coherence residual results (31% invisible structural transitions), Backblaze cross-domain validation, Compositional Governance Scale (5 levels), CoDaWork 2026 Coimbra reference, philosophical framing (CGS as cooperation scale, not just instrument scale), updated corpus size (300+ documents, 6 AI systems). New section VI on why Peterson specifically — the coupling between disciplines requires someone who speaks the language of meaning. Three asks refined: coherence residual mapping to psychology, CGS as governance version of meaning hierarchy, engagement for Coimbra/Ramsar.

Collective distribution report for April 5 governance scale session. Covers: CGS creation and renaming, coherence residual first computation, Backblaze cross-domain validation, governance state advance (CGS-1,n=2)→(CGS-2,n=3). Includes files_for_collective_review with priority tiers, ChatGPT-specific review instructions (science first, no fluff), 7 open items for collective action. Corpus: 310 files.

Abstract v3: 197 words. Opens with 'calibration study, not framework.' Three diagnostics named (TV + Aitchison + CR). CR results included (mean 0.58, 31% invisible structural). Backblaze cross-domain validation sentence. 25-error catalogue. All three alignment sentences present.

Expanded 14→16 slides. Added Slide 10 (CR results: strict coherence does not hold, 31% invisible structural, Egozcue-Greenacre question answered), Slide 11 (Backblaze cross-domain: same patterns, different physics). Slide 7 updated 17→25 errors. Closing rewritten with three alignment sentences. Elevator pitch updated with CR results and Backblaze.
Error count 17→25 with breakdown. Section 3b added: coherence residual as third diagnostic with empirical results. Architecture diagram metric layer updated: dual-metric → three-diagnostic protocol.

Strategic roadmap April 6 – June 5, 2026. 10 ranked weaknesses across 3 tiers. Week-by-week calendar: Weeks 1-2 depth (independence, thresholds, SBP sensitivity, CR simulation), Week 3 width (ecological pilot, human review), Week 4 consolidation, Weeks 5-6 results-dependent fork, Weeks 7-8 presentation polish. Depth vs width decision framework. Honest achievability assessment. What-not-to-do list.

Collective distribution report for S015. Conference alignment session: Core Stack PDF, abstract v3, presentation v2 (16 slides), three alignment sentences, folder restructure (46→27+11+8), two-month validation roadmap. Resolves 2 of 7 CRPT-008 open items (abstract v3, presentation update). Remaining 5 mapped to roadmap weeks.

19 files moved to subfolders. 11 files → post-coimbra/ (CGS, CMSI, Ramsar, Scaling, Confidence, Q-Inquisitor, Governance Declaration, CONJ-001, 2 spreadsheets, Conference Concepts). 8 files → archive/ (abstract v1, abstract v2, Executive Summary, collective moment S006, THE_CORE, translator, System Map, Peterson letter v7). No files deleted.
S015 entry finalized with folder restructure and CRPT-009.

sessions.label: Spectral independence + impulse response + dependency chain + governance charter + proof burden register + battle card + corpus revision brief
sessions.suggested_commit_message: S016: Spectral independence test (W-1), Fukushima impulse response analysis, dependency chain, HUF Governance Charter, Proof Burden Register, Battle Card, corpus revision brief for collective
sessions.notes: Triggered by Peter's Fukushima timezone question. Self-correction led to FFT spectral analysis (W-1 addressed), loudspeaker crossover analogy (carrier group delays, SBP phase alignment), dependency chain (forced relay through closure). ChatGPT co-developed: Proof Burden and Misunderstanding Register (9 items), live Battle Card (15 pressure points), HUF Governance Charter (9 articles: Governed Breakpoint Principle, Right to Interrupt Principle, E-Stop refinement). Three named principles: Scarborough Bluffs Principle, Governed Breakpoint Principle, Right to Interrupt Principle. Alignment sentence 2 updated: 'operating in different frequency bands of structural change.' Corpus revision brief proposes 27→10 document consolidation for Coimbra.

files.summary: Formal governance charter. 9 articles. Governed Breakpoint Principle (Article II) with E-Stop refinement: de-energize correction output while observation path stays live. Right to Interrupt Principle (Article III). Open-loop priority (Article IV). 6 rights preserved (Article VIII). Declaration (Article IX).

9-item register: MC-4 novelty, three diagnostics, CR grounding, null model, zeros, loudspeaker bridge, governance doctrine, Ramsar readiness, documentation volume. Each maps claim → proof burden → likely misunderstanding → safe wording. Rewrite filter: keep sentence only if it reduces misunderstanding more than it increases proof burden.

Two-column live rehearsal card. 15 pressure-point questions with calibrated answers. Safe/red-flag phrase lists. Three anchor sentences. Three named principles (Scarborough Bluffs, Governed Breakpoint, Right to Interrupt).
Title: 'Unified Command Dashboard' → 'CoDa Calibration Demonstrator'. Subtitle: 'Dual Metric Engine' → 'Three-Diagnostic Protocol (TV + Aitchison + CR)'. 5 occurrences replaced.
Collective distribution report for S016. Full session findings, revision proposal, governance charter announcement, collective conversation topics.

Collective conversation document: 8 topics requiring collective input before corpus consolidation. Wave mechanics reframing, 27→10 architecture, alignment sentence update, charter placement, register completeness, analysis output promotion, simulation design, cooperation lexicon.
S016 entry added. Pending items rewritten. File count updated.

S016 files added to review tiers (3 analysis JSONs to Tier 1, register+battle card to Tier 2, charter to Tier 3, CRPT-010 to Tier 5). Verification checklist expanded V-11 through V-14. Governance state and file count updated.

pending_items.notes: All sessions pushed. S009-S011: commit 0a621fab (2026-04-04). S012-S015: pushed by Peter 2026-04-05.

6 files staged: HUF_GOVERNANCE_CHARTER.md, PROOF_BURDEN_AND_MISUNDERSTANDING_REGISTER.md, BATTLE_CARD_LIVE.md, analyzer title fix, CRPT-010.json, STAGING_MANIFEST.json. Plus Claude CoWorker analysis outputs (spectral, impulse, dependency chain JSONs/PNGs, revision brief).

FFT spectral analysis shows three diagnostics concentrate power in different frequency bands: CR 2-3yr, Aitchison 4-12yr, TV 3-25yr. Mean |r| = 0.231 (time), 0.286 (frequency). Result is stronger than planned correlation test — shows WHY they're independent, not just that they have low correlation. See HUF_Spectral_Independence_W1.json.

Two-phase CR model established: CR correlates with group delay mismatch at impulse (r=+0.61) but not at settling (r=-0.15). SBP as filter bank interpretation. Still needs full simulation validation per roadmap Week 2.
Sweep TV, Aitchison, CR thresholds. Check if 31% STRUCTURAL is robust.
Dispatchable vs Intermittent at root. Rerun CR. Compare patterns.
'Unified Command Dashboard' → 'CoDa Calibration Demonstrator'. 'Dual Metric Engine' → 'Three-Diagnostic Protocol (TV + Aitchison + CR)'. 5 occurrences replaced in HUF_Spectrum_Analyzer_v3.html.

S016 revision brief proposes Tier 1 (3 conference-facing), Tier 2 (4 supporting), Tier 3 (3 governance). Key merge: WHAT_HUF_IS + THE_LINEAGE + THE_THIRD_DIAGNOSTIC + THE_UNION → THE_INSTRUMENT.md. ChatGPT to guide rewrite. See HUF_CHATGPT_REVISION_BRIEF_S016.json.
Charter created at repo root. Must be cross-referenced in consolidated documents. E-Stop refinement (Article II), Right to Interrupt (Article III), 6 rights preserved (Article VIII).
ChatGPT recommended three-level vocabulary ladder: (1) native CoDa, (2) bridge terms, (3) HUF-specific. Universal translator for the data disassembler. See revision brief.
Packet v3 predates CR results and spectral independence findings. May need v4 incorporating third diagnostic, W-1 results, and impulse response analysis.
Engineering complement to chiPower. E-03/E-17 as collaborative calibration.
